approvals.rationale: The coordinated W2/W3 transition establishes the W3-owned CapabilitySpec as the single canonical runtime and codec identity; retaining the W2 declaration would preserve a forbidden duplicate authority model.
tests: test/authority/canonical-facet-transition.test.ts#coordinated FacetRef and CapabilitySpec transition [C13-AUTH-ROLE-MATERIALIZATION] retains the exact W3 CapabilitySpec through Role and Grant runtime records
test/authority/canonical-facet-transition.test.ts#coordinated FacetRef and CapabilitySpec transition [C13-AUTH-BINDING-RESOLUTION] retains the exact W3 FacetRef through Binding runtime records

approvals.rationale: The reviewed W1 split moves ContentStat to its own module and retains media, owner-edge retention, and transient lease records as separate concepts, eliminating the mixed legacy record module without dropping behavior.
tests: test/content/records.test.ts#content record codecs [content.stat] [content.owner-edge] [content.transient-lease] round-trips actual fixtures with their RecordCodec kinds
test/content/records.test.ts#content record codecs content stat rejects every malformed field and invalid value
test/content/records.test.ts#content value contracts models active, released, expired, and idempotently closed lease orders

approvals.rationale: The reviewed W1 split gives ContentRef, Revision, and SecretRef independent modules and exposes those exact identities through the core and package barrels; the combined value module is no longer canonical.
tests: test/core/content-ref.test.ts#ContentRef round-trips a SHA-256 content address through a detached digest
test/core/revision.test.ts#Revision increments without mutating the prior value
test/core/secret-ref.test.ts#SecretRef validates and compares structured references

approvals.rationale: SemVer is now the exact W1-owned semantic-version value and strict codec; removing the generic version module prevents the retired mixed compatibility surface from surviving beside it.
tests: test/core/semver.test.ts#SemVer parses and renders the complete SemVer 2.0.0 form
test/core/semver.test.ts#SemVer [core.semver] round-trips deterministically through its strict codec
